description: After filing an ITR, it must be verified within 30 days. Unverified ITRs are treated as if not filed. E-verification is the preferred electronic method. Physical ITR-V can be sent to CPC Bangalore as alternative.
timeline: Within 30 days of filing date (CBDT Notification No. 5/2023, effective August 1, 2022)

steps.description: Go to www.incometax.gov.in → Login → e-Verify Return

Options: (a) Aadhaar OTP — OTP sent to Aadhaar-linked mobile; (b) Net banking EVC — generate EVC via bank; (c) Bank account EVC — pre-validated bank account; (d) Demat account EVC; (e) DSC (Digital Signature Certificate) — mandatory for companies and audit cases; (f) ITR-V physical submission to CPC Bangalore.
For Aadhaar OTP: Enter 6-digit OTP received on Aadhaar-registered mobile. For bank EVC: Copy EVC from bank's net banking portal and enter on income tax portal.
After successful e-verification, portal shows success message. Acknowledgment email sent to registered email. Return moves to 'Verified' status.

alternatives.description: Print signed ITR-V (1-page acknowledgment from portal), sign in BLUE ink, send by ordinary post or speed post to: CPC, Post Box No. 1, Electronic City Post Office, Bengaluru – 560 100. Must reach within 30 days of filing.
alternatives.applicableTo: Only for ITR-1 to ITR-4 filed without DSC (not applicable for companies, audit cases)

commonMistakes: Not verifying within 30 days — return treated as never filed, leading to late filing consequences
Sending ITR-V by courier/registered post (only ordinary or speed post accepted)
Using DSC of wrong person (company must use authorized signatory's DSC)
Aadhaar OTP not generating because Aadhaar not linked to mobile number
consequences: Unverified ITR is invalid — treated as non-filing
Interest u/s 234A applicable as if return not filed
Carry-forward of losses forfeited if return not verified timely
Refund not processed until verification complete
legalReference: Section 140, Rule 12; CBDT Notification No. 5/2023 dated July 29, 2022
